title: Allow using `podman image scp` with a user with an `@` in the name
body:
### Feature request description

I would like to be able to copy an image built with my regular user to the root user's storage, but my computer is joined to an Active Directory domain and we use the fully qualified syntax `user@domain`:

```
sudo podman image scp aduser@domain@localhost::example.com/foo/bar:latest
```

But I get this error:

```
Error: user: unknown user aduser
```

### Suggest potential solution

Parse the `@` in the string differently, i.e. use the last `@` before `::` instead of the first. I couldn't find the place where parsing the source string takes place in Podman -- I got as far as [this function](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/3681055601c5c2a882fc6773a2fc72ef6eb1dadf/pkg/bindings/images/images.go#L327) and gave up.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I know skopeo is more powerful, but I've never used it. From the look at `skopeo-copy(1)`, it seems to handle usernames the same.

comments.body:
oddly the man page suggests names can be used

```
       $ podman image scp charliedoern@192.168.68.126:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock::alpine
       WARN[0000] Unknown connection name given. Please use system connection add to specify the default remote socket location
       Getting image source signatures
       Copying blob 9450ef9feb15 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
       Copying config 1f97f0559c done
       Writing manifest to image destination
       Storing signatures
       Loaded image: docker.io/library/alpine:latest
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@baude hey, 
was looking for this issue and found few things that might help in making up a pr for this if the approach is write acc. to u please do assign this issue or give me a thumbs up to move forward to proceed with it 
root cause what i feel is in ParseImageSCPArg the @ localhost:: branch splits the user on the first @ ...  so  the aduser@domain gets cut to aduser and the lookup fails.
Cutting on @ localhost:: instead would keep the full user@domain and leave the remote user@host:: path untouched
Happy to add a scp_test.go case for this if you want

@baude https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/compare/main...ROKUMATE:podman:fix-image-scp-at-username
i have implemented a fix im my branch will create a pr on your approvals 👍🏼
